Test Case: PHASE 2 — OPERATIONAL ACCEPTANCE TESTING: Daily Schedule + Production Entry
Phase 2 migrate runs cleanly on Phase 1 site
Steps: bench --site [site] migrate after Phase 2 code deployed
Expected Result: Zero errors. DPS, Schedule Line, Production Entry, Downtime Entry tables created

Steps: Run bench migrate twice
Expected Result: No errors on second run. No duplicate data

Test Case: Existing Phase 1 data intact after migrate
Steps: Check Production Stage, Waste Reason, Downtime Reason records
Expected Result: All Phase 1 master data unchanged

Steps: Open previously created job cards
Expected Result: All data preserved. New custom fields show Not Started

Test Case: DPS list loads under 1s with 100+ records
Steps: Create 100 DPS records, measure list load time
Expected Result: Page load < 1 second

Test Case: DPS form loads under 1s with 20 schedule lines
Steps: Open a DPS with 20 child rows
Expected Result: Form load < 1 second

Test Case: Production Entry list loads under 1s with 500+ records
Steps: Create 500 PE records, measure list load time
Expected Result: Page load < 1 second

Test Case: Schedule Board loads under 2s with 10 machines
Steps: Open Schedule Board for a date with 10 machines and 5 jobs each
Expected Result: Board renders in < 2 seconds

Steps: Drag a card to a new position, measure API response time
Expected Result: Server round-trip < 500ms. Card settles in new position without visible delay

Steps: Submit a PE, measure time for job card production_status update
Expected Result: Job card updated within 500ms of PE submit

Test Case: Daily Production Summary report under 3s for 30-day range
Steps: Run report with 30-day date range and 1000+ PE records
Expected Result: Report renders in < 3 seconds

Test Case: Job Progress report under 3s with 200+ job cards
Steps: Run report with no filters on a site with 200+ job cards
Expected Result: Report renders in < 3 seconds

Test Case: DPS unique constraint enforced under concurrency
Steps: Two users simultaneously try to create DPS for same machine+date
Expected Result: Only one succeeds. Second gets clear duplicate error

Steps: Submit PE, verify job card update in same DB transaction
Expected Result: If PE submit fails mid-way, job card is not partially updated

Steps: Submit 3 PEs (total 9000), cancel 1 (3000)
Expected Result: Job card shows 6000. production_status correct for the new total

Steps: Try to delete a submitted Production Entry
Expected Result: Frappe blocks deletion of submitted documents

Test Case: DPS deletion blocked if PEs reference it
Steps: Try to delete a DPS that has Production Entries linked via daily_production_schedule
Expected Result: Deletion blocked with LinkExistsError

Test Case: Cancelling job card blocks new PEs
Steps: Cancel a job card, then try to create a PE referencing it
Expected Result: PE validate() rejects with error: Cannot create Production Entry for a cancelled Job Card

Steps: Reorder via board: items at 10, 20, 30 become 10, 30, 20
Expected Result: sequence_order values updated correctly. No duplicates

Test Case: DPS totals recalculate on line add/remove
Steps: Add a Schedule Line then delete one, check totals
Expected Result: total_planned_qty, total_planned_hours, utilization_pct all recalculated

Steps: User A and User B both open the same DPS, User A saves first, User B tries to save
Expected Result: User B gets TimestampMismatchError (Frappe optimistic locking). Prompted to reload

Test Case: Two planners use Schedule Board for same date
Steps: User A drags a card while User B adds a job to the same machine
Expected Result: Both operations succeed without data loss. Board reflects both changes on refresh

Test Case: Operator submits PE while planner edits DPS
Steps: Operator submits PE linked to a DPS that planner has open
Expected Result: PE submits successfully. Planner sees updated data on next save/reload

Steps: GET /api/resource/Daily Production Schedule/DPS-2026-00001
Expected Result: JSON response includes all fields and schedule_lines child table

Steps: POST /api/resource/Production Entry with valid payload
Expected Result: PE created. Returns 200 with document data

Test Case: Schedule Board API endpoints respond correctly
Steps: Call the frappe.call endpoints used by the board JS
Expected Result: Returns DPS data grouped by workstation for the requested date

Steps: GET /api/method/frappe.client.get_report with report=Daily Production Summary
Expected Result: Returns report data in expected format

Steps: Open Schedule Board on Chrome (latest)
Expected Result: All interactions work: drag, click, filter, date navigation

Steps: Open Schedule Board on Firefox (latest)
Expected Result: All interactions work

Steps: Open Schedule Board on Safari (latest)
Expected Result: All interactions work

Steps: Open Schedule Board on Edge (latest)
Expected Result: All interactions work

Steps: Open Schedule Board on iPad or 768px viewport
Expected Result: Layout adapts. Columns scrollable or tabbed. Touch drag works

Steps: Open Production Entry form on mobile phone
Expected Result: All fields accessible. Form submittable

Test Case: No JS console errors on any page
Steps: Open each new page/form, check browser console across browsers
Expected Result: Zero JS errors

Steps: GET /api/resource/Daily Production Schedule without session
Expected Result: 403 Forbidden

Test Case: CSRF protection on Schedule Board mutations
Steps: Board drag-and-drop API calls include X-Frappe-CSRF-Token
Expected Result: Requests without valid CSRF token are rejected

Steps: Login as Guest, try to submit PE via API
Expected Result: 403 Forbidden

Test Case: SQL injection safe on report filters
Steps: Pass malicious input in report date filter (e.g., 2026-01-01'; DROP TABLE--)
Expected Result: Query parameterized. No SQL injection. Report returns empty or error

Test Case: XSS safe on Schedule Board notes
Steps: Enter <script>alert(1)</script> in Schedule Line notes field
Expected Result: Script tags escaped in board card rendering. No alert fires

Steps: Run bench backup, inspect SQL dump
Expected Result: Tables for DPS, Schedule Line, Production Entry, Downtime Entry present

Steps: Restore backup to fresh site
Expected Result: All DPS, PE, DT records intact. Schedule Board renders correctly

Test Case: App uninstall removes Phase 2 doctypes cleanly
Steps: bench uninstall-app production_log
Expected Result: Phase 2 tables removed. No orphan references in job cards (custom fields also removed)

Steps: Uninstall then re-install production_log
Expected Result: Clean install. All fixtures reload. No migration errors

Test Case: PE submit logged in audit trail
Steps: Submit a PE, check Version history on the PE document
Expected Result: Version log shows submit action with timestamp and user

Steps: Check Version history on a job card after PE submit changes status
Expected Result: Version log shows production_status change from Not Started to In Progress

Steps: Drag a card on the board, check DPS Version history
Expected Result: Version log shows schedule_lines modification with timestamp

Test Case: Error logging on failed PE submit
Steps: Trigger a PE validation error, check error log
Expected Result: Error logged in Frappe Error Log with full traceback
